nfhs_plain_label: Women age 15+ with mildly high (Stage 1) blood pressure (systolic 140-159 mmHg and/or diastolic 90-99 mmHg) (%)
symptoms: usually no symptoms|occasional headaches|mild dizziness
edge_basis: Stage 1 HTN is commonly asymptomatic; managed first-line in primary care (internal/family med), cardiology for risk stratification.

nfhs_plain_label: Women age 15+ with moderately or severely high (Stage 2) blood pressure (systolic >=160 mmHg and/or diastolic >=100 mmHg) (%)
symptoms: severe headache|chest discomfort|shortness of breath|blurred vision|nosebleeds
edge_basis: Stage 2 HTN: higher cardiovascular/renal/stroke risk, cardiology-led; nephrology for renal involvement/secondary causes.

nfhs_plain_label: Women age 15+ with high blood pressure (systolic >=140 mmHg and/or diastolic >=90 mmHg) or on medication (%)
symptoms: often no symptoms|headache|dizziness|shortness of breath
edge_basis: Composite any-HTN prevalence; often asymptomatic. Primary care + cardiology standard management.

nfhs_plain_label: Men age 15+ with mildly high (Stage 1) blood pressure (systolic 140-159 mmHg and/or diastolic 90-99 mmHg) (%)
symptoms: usually no symptoms|occasional headaches|mild dizziness
edge_basis: Stage 1 HTN is commonly asymptomatic; managed first-line in primary care, cardiology for risk stratification.

nfhs_plain_label: Men age 15+ with moderately or severely high (Stage 2) blood pressure (systolic >=160 mmHg and/or diastolic >=100 mmHg) (%)
symptoms: severe headache|chest discomfort|shortness of breath|blurred vision|nosebleeds
edge_basis: Stage 2 HTN: higher cardiovascular/renal/stroke risk, cardiology-led; nephrology for renal involvement/secondary causes.

nfhs_plain_label: Men age 15+ with high blood pressure (systolic >=140 mmHg and/or diastolic >=90 mmHg) or on medication (%)
symptoms: often no symptoms|headache|dizziness|shortness of breath
edge_basis: Composite any-HTN prevalence; often asymptomatic. Primary care + cardiology standard management.

nfhs_plain_label: Women age 15+ with high (141-160 mg/dL) random blood sugar level (%)
symptoms: increased thirst|frequent urination|constant tiredness|blurred vision
edge_basis: Elevated random glucose flags impaired glucose regulation/early diabetes; endocrinology + primary care.

nfhs_plain_label: Women age 15+ with very high (>160 mg/dL) random blood sugar level (%)
symptoms: excessive thirst|frequent urination|unexplained weight loss|extreme tiredness|blurred vision|slow-healing sores
edge_basis: Very high glucose indicates likely diabetes; endocrinology-led, nephrology for diabetic kidney complications.

nfhs_plain_label: Women age 15+ with high or very high (>140 mg/dL) random blood sugar level or on medicine to control blood sugar (%)
symptoms: increased thirst|frequent urination|fatigue|blurred vision|tingling in hands or feet
edge_basis: Composite any-diabetes prevalence (>140 mg/dL or on meds); endocrinology + primary care.

nfhs_plain_label: Men age 15+ with high (141-160 mg/dL) random blood sugar level (%)
symptoms: increased thirst|frequent urination|constant tiredness|blurred vision
edge_basis: Elevated random glucose flags impaired glucose regulation/early diabetes; endocrinology + primary care.

nfhs_plain_label: Men age 15+ with very high (>160 mg/dL) random blood sugar level (%)
symptoms: excessive thirst|frequent urination|unexplained weight loss|extreme tiredness|blurred vision|slow-healing sores
edge_basis: Very high glucose indicates likely diabetes; endocrinology-led, nephrology for diabetic kidney complications.

nfhs_plain_label: Men age 15+ with high or very high (>140 mg/dL) random blood sugar level or on medicine to control blood sugar (%)
symptoms: increased thirst|frequent urination|fatigue|blurred vision|tingling in hands or feet
edge_basis: Composite any-diabetes prevalence (>140 mg/dL or on meds); endocrinology + primary care.

nfhs_plain_label: Women age 15-49 years who are underweight (BMI < 18.5 kg/m2) (%)
symptoms: low body weight|constant tiredness|weakness|feeling cold|hair thinning
edge_basis: Underweight is a nutritional risk factor; primary care first-line, gastroenterology if malabsorption suspected.

nfhs_plain_label: Women age 15-49 years who are overweight or obese (BMI >= 25.0 kg/m2) (%)
symptoms: excess body weight|breathlessness on exertion|joint pain|low energy
edge_basis: Obesity is a modifiable metabolic risk factor; lifestyle medicine first-line, endocrinology for metabolic workup, bariatric surgery for severe obesity.

nfhs_plain_label: Women age 15-49 years who have a high-risk waist-to-hip ratio (WHR >= 0.85) (%)
symptoms: increased waist size|no specific symptoms
edge_basis: High WHR = central obesity, a cardiometabolic risk marker (asymptomatic); lifestyle medicine + endocrinology, cardiology for CV risk.

nfhs_plain_label: Women age 15+ who use any kind of tobacco (%)
symptoms: cravings for tobacco|persistent cough|shortness of breath|stained teeth|mouth sores
edge_basis: Tobacco use is a leading modifiable risk factor; preventive medicine/cessation, pulmonology for respiratory effects, psychiatry/addiction for dependence.

nfhs_plain_label: Men age 15+ who use any kind of tobacco (%)
symptoms: cravings for tobacco|persistent cough|shortness of breath|stained teeth|mouth sores
edge_basis: Tobacco use is a leading modifiable risk factor; preventive medicine/cessation, pulmonology for respiratory effects, psychiatry/addiction for dependence.

nfhs_plain_label: Women age 15+ who consume alcohol (%)
symptoms: strong urge to drink|trouble cutting down|nausea|abdominal pain|low mood
edge_basis: Alcohol use is a modifiable risk factor; psychiatry/addiction for use disorder, gastroenterology/hepatology for liver effects, preventive medicine for screening.

nfhs_plain_label: Men age 15+ who consume alcohol (%)
symptoms: strong urge to drink|trouble cutting down|nausea|abdominal pain|low mood
edge_basis: Alcohol use is a modifiable risk factor; psychiatry/addiction for use disorder, gastroenterology/hepatology for liver effects, preventive medicine for screening.

nfhs_plain_label: Children 6-59 months who are anaemic (Hb <11.0 g/dl) (%)
condition_or_topic: Childhood anaemia (iron-deficiency / nutritional anaemia in young children)
symptoms: child looks pale|child always tired|child not eating well|child gets tired quickly|child breathless when playing|pale lips and nails
edge_basis: MODELED: childhood anaemia presents as pallor/fatigue; primary pediatric care, hematology for workup of severe/refractory cases

nfhs_plain_label: Non-pregnant women 15-49 yrs who are anaemic (Hb <12.0 g/dl) (%)
condition_or_topic: Anaemia in non-pregnant women of reproductive age (iron-deficiency anaemia)
symptoms: constant tiredness|looking pale|feeling weak|shortness of breath on exertion|dizziness|heavy periods|headaches
edge_basis: MODELED: anaemia in reproductive-age women → fatigue/pallor; internal medicine first-line, hematology for workup, gynecology for menstrual blood-loss cause

nfhs_plain_label: Pregnant women 15-49 yrs who are anaemic (Hb <11.0 g/dl) (%)
symptoms: tiredness during pregnancy|looking pale while pregnant|feeling weak in pregnancy|breathless when walking|dizziness|fast heartbeat
edge_basis: MODELED: anaemia in pregnancy managed in antenatal/obstetric care; MFM for high-risk/severe cases, hematology for refractory workup

nfhs_plain_label: All women 15-49 yrs who are anaemic (%)
condition_or_topic: Anaemia in women of reproductive age (overall)
symptoms: constant tiredness|looking pale|feeling weak|breathlessness|dizziness|low energy
edge_basis: MODELED: overall reproductive-age female anaemia; internal/family medicine + ob-gyn (includes pregnant subset), hematology for workup

nfhs_plain_label: All women/adolescent girls 15-19 yrs who are anaemic (%)
symptoms: always tired|looking pale|feeling weak|tired at school|breathless climbing stairs|poor concentration|heavy periods
edge_basis: MODELED: adolescent (15-19) anaemia straddles pediatric/adolescent and adult internal medicine; hematology for workup

nfhs_plain_label: Children under 5 yrs who are stunted (low height-for-age) (%)
symptoms: child not growing tall|child shorter than other kids|child not growing|delayed growth|child smaller for age
edge_basis: MODELED: stunting = chronic undernutrition; pediatrics primary, pediatric endocrinology for growth assessment, GI for malabsorption causes

nfhs_plain_label: Children under 5 yrs who are wasted (low weight-for-height) (%)
symptoms: child very thin|child losing weight|child looks frail|child not gaining weight|ribs showing
edge_basis: MODELED: wasting = acute undernutrition; pediatrics primary, peds critical care for complicated SAM, GI for underlying cause

nfhs_plain_label: Children under 5 yrs who are severely wasted (severe low weight-for-height) (%)
condition_or_topic: Severe acute malnutrition / severe wasting in under-5 children
symptoms: child extremely thin|child very weak|child not eating|child losing a lot of weight|child looks skin and bones|child listless
edge_basis: MODELED: severe wasting (SAM) is life-threatening, needs inpatient/critical pediatric care; neonatology relevant for youngest infants

nfhs_plain_label: Children under 5 yrs who are underweight (low weight-for-age) (%)
symptoms: child underweight|child not gaining weight|child too thin for age|child not growing|poor appetite
edge_basis: MODELED: underweight = composite undernutrition; pediatrics primary, GI/endocrine for underlying malabsorption/metabolic causes

nfhs_plain_label: Children under 5 yrs who are overweight (high weight-for-height) (%)
symptoms: child gaining too much weight|child overweight|child too heavy for age|child chubby and not active
edge_basis: MODELED: early childhood overweight; pediatrics primary, peds endocrine for metabolic workup, lifestyle medicine for prevention

nfhs_plain_label: Children under 6 months exclusively breastfed (%)
edge_basis: MODELED care_gap: low exclusive-breastfeeding coverage is the deficit; addressed by pediatric/neonatal feeding support and postnatal maternity lactation counseling

nfhs_plain_label: Children 6-23 months receiving an adequate diet (%)
condition_or_topic: Adequate complementary feeding/diet coverage (infant & young child feeding)
edge_basis: MODELED care_gap: low adequate-diet coverage is the deficit; pediatric nutrition + preventive medicine address IYCF; GI for feeding/malabsorption concerns

nfhs_plain_label: Households using iodized salt (%)
condition_or_topic: Iodized salt usage coverage (iodine deficiency prevention)
edge_basis: MODELED care_gap: low iodized-salt coverage = population iodine-deficiency risk (goiter, child cognitive impairment); preventive medicine primary, endocrinology for thyroid/iodine disorders

nfhs_plain_label: Children under 5 years who had diarrhoea in the 2 weeks before the survey (%)
symptoms: child has diarrhoea|child has loose watery stools|child dehydrated|child vomiting|child fever
edge_basis: MODELED: acute diarrhoea in u5 presents as loose stools/dehydration; managed by pediatrics, GI for severe/persistent, general medicine where no pediatrician.

nfhs_plain_label: Children under 5 with diarrhoea who received oral rehydration salts (ORS) (%)
condition_or_topic: ORS treatment coverage for childhood diarrhoea
edge_basis: MODELED: ORS is the frontline rehydration therapy delivered by pediatric/primary/emergency care; low coverage = care gap in those services.

nfhs_plain_label: Children under 5 with diarrhoea who received zinc (%)
condition_or_topic: Zinc treatment coverage for childhood diarrhoea
edge_basis: MODELED: zinc is WHO-recommended adjunct to ORS for u5 diarrhoea, prescribed in pediatric/GI/primary care; low coverage = treatment gap.

nfhs_plain_label: Children under 5 with diarrhoea taken to a health facility or health provider (%)
edge_basis: MODELED: facility care-seeking for diarrhoea routes to pediatric/primary care and emergency for dehydration; low % = access/utilization gap.

nfhs_plain_label: Children under 5 who had symptoms of acute respiratory infection (ARI) in the 2 weeks before the survey (%)
symptoms: child coughing and breathing fast|child has trouble breathing|child fever|child chest indrawing|child runny nose
edge_basis: MODELED: ARI/pneumonia in u5 presents as cough + fast/difficult breathing + fever; pediatrics primary, pulmonology and ID for severe/infectious cause.

nfhs_plain_label: Children under 5 with fever or symptoms of ARI taken to a health facility or provider (%)
edge_basis: MODELED: fever/ARI care-seeking routes to pediatric/primary care and emergency for respiratory distress; low % = access/utilization gap.

nfhs_plain_label: Children 12-23 months fully vaccinated (per vaccination card/record) (%)
edge_basis: MODELED: routine immunization is delivered by pediatric/primary/preventive services; low full-coverage = immunization care gap.

nfhs_plain_label: Children 12-23 months who received BCG vaccine (%)
edge_basis: MODELED: BCG protects against TB, given at birth via pediatric/preventive immunization; ID relevant to the TB target. Low % = vaccine gap.

nfhs_plain_label: Children 12-23 months who received 3 doses of polio vaccine (%)
edge_basis: MODELED: polio series delivered by pediatric/preventive immunization; ID relevant to vaccine-preventable infection. Low % = vaccine gap.

nfhs_plain_label: Children 12-23 months who received 3 doses of pentavalent or DPT vaccine (%)
edge_basis: MODELED: penta/DPT (diphtheria-pertussis-tetanus +) series delivered by pediatric/preventive immunization; ID relevant. Low % = vaccine gap.

nfhs_plain_label: Children 12-23 months who received the first dose of measles-containing vaccine (MCV1) (%)
edge_basis: MODELED: MCV1 prevents measles, delivered by pediatric/preventive immunization; ID relevant to outbreak-prone infection. Low % = vaccine gap.

nfhs_plain_label: Children 12-23 months who received 3 doses of rotavirus vaccine (%)
edge_basis: MODELED: rotavirus vaccine prevents severe infant diarrhoea, delivered by pediatric/preventive immunization; GI relevant to the GI-pathogen target. Low % = vaccine gap.

nfhs_plain_label: Children 9-35 months who received a vitamin A dose in the last 6 months (%)
edge_basis: MODELED: periodic vitamin A supplementation is a preventive child-health intervention delivered by pediatric/primary/preventive services. Low % = supplementation gap.

nfhs_plain_label: Mothers who had an antenatal care (ANC) visit in the first trimester (%)
edge_basis: Early ANC is delivered by obstetric/maternity providers; low first-trimester ANC coverage signals unmet demand for routine and high-risk antenatal services.

nfhs_plain_label: Mothers who had at least 4 antenatal care (ANC) visits (%)
condition_or_topic: Antenatal care coverage (>=4 ANC visits / WHO schedule)
edge_basis: Adequate ANC visit count is an obstetric/maternity service; low coverage indicates gap in routine antenatal monitoring and risk detection.

nfhs_plain_label: Mothers who received postnatal care (PNC) from a doctor/nurse/LHV/ANM/midwife within 2 days of delivery (%)
condition_or_topic: Postnatal care coverage by a skilled provider
edge_basis: Skilled PNC is provided by maternity/obstetric staff (neonatal overlap for newborn care); low coverage = gap in early postpartum/newborn surveillance for hemorrhage, sepsis, etc.

nfhs_plain_label: Institutional births in the 5 years before the survey (%)
edge_basis: Institutional birth requires facility-based maternity/obstetric capacity; low coverage signals demand for delivery services and emergency obstetric care.

nfhs_plain_label: Births attended by skilled health personnel in the 5 years before the survey (%)
edge_basis: Skilled birth attendance is an obstetric/midwifery service (neonatal overlap for newborn resuscitation); low coverage = gap in safe delivery workforce.

nfhs_plain_label: Births delivered by C-section in the 5 years before the survey (%)
condition_or_topic: Cesarean section rate (both under- and over-utilization are harmful)
edge_basis: DIRECTION=context: C-section rate is U-shaped - too LOW means lack of emergency obstetric/surgical access (untreated obstructed labor), too HIGH means overuse/unnecessary surgery. Not a simple low_pct_bad coverage gap; specialty stays obstetric, interpret against WHO ~10-15% benchmark, hence low confidence.

nfhs_plain_label: Mothers who consumed iron-folic acid (IFA) for 180 days or more when they were pregnant (%)
condition_or_topic: Antenatal iron-folic acid supplementation (anemia prevention in pregnancy)
edge_basis: IFA supplementation is part of antenatal care delivered by maternity/obstetric providers; low coverage reflects gap in the anemia-prevention component of ANC.

nfhs_plain_label: Current use of any modern family planning method, currently married women age 15-49 (%)
edge_basis: Modern contraception is provided by family-planning / reproductive-health services; low uptake indicates demand for contraceptive counseling and provision.

nfhs_plain_label: Total unmet need for family planning, currently married women age 15-49 (%)
edge_basis: Unmet need directly quantifies the family-planning service gap; HIGH unmet need = worse, so direction is high_pct_bad. Served by FP/contraception and reproductive-health providers.

nfhs_plain_label: Women age 30-49 who have ever undergone a cervical (cancer) screening (%)
edge_basis: Cervical screening detects cervical cancer; diagnosis/treatment falls to obstetric-gynecology and gyn/medical oncology. Low screening coverage = undetected disease and unmet oncologic demand.

nfhs_plain_label: Women age 30-49 who have ever undergone a breast examination (%)
condition_or_topic: Breast cancer screening (clinical breast exam) coverage
edge_basis: Breast exam screens for breast cancer; diagnosis/treatment falls to surgical and medical oncology, with gynecology/obstetrics as a common screening entry point. Low coverage = undetected disease and unmet oncologic demand.

nfhs_plain_label: Women age 30-49 who have ever undergone an oral cancer examination (%)
edge_basis: Oral cancer screening detects oral/oropharyngeal malignancy; diagnosis/treatment falls to otolaryngology and oral & maxillofacial surgery, with dentistry as a common screening/detection point. Low coverage = undetected disease.

nfhs_plain_label: Households using an improved sanitation facility (%)
condition_or_topic: Improved sanitation access (social determinant of health)
edge_basis: No direct clinical specialty: sanitation is an environmental/infrastructure determinant, not a diagnosis or procedure. INDIRECT only - poor sanitation raises diarrhoeal/enteric and infectious disease burden (would touch infectiousdiseases/pediatrics downstream), but that is a population-health effect, not a facility-matching specialty route.

nfhs_plain_label: Households with an improved drinking-water source (%)
condition_or_topic: Improved drinking water access (social determinant of health)
edge_basis: No direct clinical specialty: water access is infrastructure, not a clinical service. INDIRECT only - unsafe water increases waterborne/diarrhoeal and infectious disease burden (infectiousdiseases/pediatrics downstream), but this is not a specialty-demand mapping.

nfhs_plain_label: Households with electricity (%)
edge_basis: No direct or specific clinical specialty: electrification is a development/infrastructure indicator. INDIRECT/diffuse only - it enables cold chain, facility lighting, and medical equipment, but cannot be attributed to any single specialty demand.

nfhs_plain_label: Households using clean fuel for cooking (%)
condition_or_topic: Clean cooking fuel access (household air pollution determinant)
edge_basis: No direct clinical specialty: cooking fuel is an environmental exposure, not a clinical service. INDIRECT only - solid-fuel smoke is a real risk factor for COPD/respiratory disease and childhood pneumonia (pulmonology/pediatrics downstream), but exposure prevalence is not a specialty-demand signal for facility matching.

nfhs_plain_label: Households with any member covered by a health insurance/financing scheme (%)
edge_basis: No clinical specialty: insurance coverage is a health-financing/access determinant affecting affordability across ALL specialties, not demand for any one. INDIRECT - low coverage suppresses care-seeking broadly, but maps to no specific specialty.

nfhs_plain_label: Women age 15-49 who are literate (%)
edge_basis: No clinical specialty: literacy is an educational/social determinant. INDIRECT - higher female literacy correlates with better maternal/child health-seeking and outcomes, but it is a contextual modifier, not a route to any specialty.

nfhs_plain_label: Women age 15-49 with 10 or more years of schooling (%)
condition_or_topic: Female educational attainment (social determinant of health)
edge_basis: No clinical specialty: years of schooling is an educational/social determinant. INDIRECT - associated with improved reproductive/child health behaviors and lower fertility, but contextual, not a specialty-demand signal.

nfhs_plain_label: Women age 20-24 married before age 18 years (%)
condition_or_topic: Child marriage prevalence (social determinant; reproductive-health risk factor)
edge_basis: No direct clinical specialty: child marriage is a social/legal determinant, so high_pct_bad. INDIRECT - it is a real upstream risk factor for early/high-risk pregnancy and maternal morbidity (obstetric burden downstream), but the indicator itself is contextual and not a facility-matching specialty route.

nfhs_plain_label: Women age 15-19 who were already mothers or pregnant at the time of the survey (%)
edge_basis: Borderline but classified context_only: this is a demographic prevalence/risk indicator (high_pct_bad), not a coverage gap. INDIRECT but strong - high teen pregnancy implies real demand for adolescent obstetric and family-planning services (gynecologyandobstetrics / familyplanningandcomplexcontraception downstream); flagged as indirect, not placed in the specialties column per the context-only contract.

nfhs_plain_label: Women age 15-24 who use a hygienic method of menstrual protection (%)
condition_or_topic: Menstrual hygiene practice (social determinant / WASH-adjacent behavior)
edge_basis: No direct clinical specialty: menstrual hygiene method use is a behavioral/WASH and affordability determinant, not a clinical service. INDIRECT - poor menstrual hygiene is associated with reproductive-tract/urinary infection risk (gynecology downstream), but the indicator is contextual.

nfhs_plain_label: Sex ratio at birth (females per 1,000 males) for births in the last 5 years
condition_or_topic: Sex ratio at birth (gender-equity / prenatal sex-selection indicator)
edge_basis: No clinical specialty: this is a population gender-equity indicator (a low female-per-male ratio signals sex-selective practices, hence low_pct_bad - fewer females = worse). NOTE: this is a ratio per 1000, not a percentage. INDIRECT/societal only - reflects gender bias and (illegal) sex selection, not demand for any clinical specialty.

nfhs_plain_label: Female population age 6 years and above who ever attended school (%)
condition_or_topic: Female school attendance / educational access (social determinant of health)
edge_basis: No clinical specialty: ever-schooled is an educational/social determinant. INDIRECT - female education correlates with better health literacy and maternal/child outcomes, but it is contextual, not a route to any specialty.
